Container.Name: TwitchMinerGo
Container.Overview:
A high-performance Go rewrite of the Twitch Channel Points Miner v2. Mines channel points, claims bonuses, places predictions, joins raids, claims drops, and more — all with a fraction of the resource usage (~5–15 MB RAM vs ~80–120 MB for the Python version).
Features: multi-account support, channel points mining, predictions with configurable strategies (SMART, HIGH_ODDS, MOST_VOTED, etc.), drops, raids, community moments, category watcher, notifications (Telegram, Discord, Webhook, Matrix, Pushover, Gotify), and a built-in analytics web UI.
Configuration: place one YAML file per account in the configs volume. The filename (without extension) becomes the Twitch username. Auth tokens and notification secrets are injected via environment variables.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the analytics web UI and health endpoint

Config.Name: Configs Volume
Config.Description: Directory for per-account YAML config files (filename without extension = Twitch username)

Config.Name: Data Volume
Config.Description: Persistent data directory for cookies and state (set DATA_DIR to /data)

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Path inside the container where cookies and state are stored

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: HTTP server port for the analytics web UI and health endpoint

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Log level: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR
